    14â†’// Package tsdb implements a time series storage for float64 sample data.
    15â†’package tsdb
    16â†’
    17â†’import (
    18â†’	"context"
    19â†’	"errors"
    20â†’	"fmt"

    34â†’	"github.com/prometheus/client_golang/prometheus"
    35â†’	"github.com/prometheus/common/promslog"
    36â†’	"go.uber.org/atomic"
    37â†’	"golang.org/x/sync/errgroup"
    38â†’
    39â†’	"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/config"
    40â†’	"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/model/labels"
    41â†’	"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/storage"
    42â†’	"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/tsdb/chunkenc"
    43â†’	"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/tsdb/chunks"
    44â†’	"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/tsdb/fileutil"
    45â†’	_ "github.com/prometheus/prometheus/tsdb/goversion" // Load the package into main to make sure minimum Go version is met.
    46â†’	"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/tsdb/tsdbutil"
    47â†’	"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/tsdb/wlog"
    48â†’	"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/util/compression"
    49â†’	"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/util/features"
    50â†’	prom_runtime "github.com/prometheus/prometheus/util/runtime"
    51â†’)
    52â†’
    53â†’const (
    54â†’	// DefaultBlockDuration in milliseconds.
    55â†’	DefaultBlockDuration = int64(2 * time.Hour / time.Millisecond)
    56â†’
    57â†’	// DefaultCompactionDelayMaxPercent in percentage.
    58â†’	DefaultCompactionDelayMaxPercent = 10
    59â†’
    60â†’	// Block dir suffixes to make deletion and creation operations atomic.
    61â†’	// We decided to do suffixes instead of creating meta.json as last (or delete as first) one,
    62â†’	// because in error case you still can recover meta.json from the block content within local TSDB dir.
    63â†’	// TODO(bwplotka): TSDB can end up with various .tmp files (e.g meta.json.tmp, WAL or segment tmp file. Think
    64â†’	// about removing those too on start to save space. Currently only blocks tmp dirs are removed.
    65â†’	tmpForDeletionBlockDirSuffix = ".tmp-for-deletion"
    66â†’	tmpForCreationBlockDirSuffix = ".tmp-for-creation"
    67â†’	// Pre-2.21 tmp dir suffix, used in clean-up functions.
    68â†’	tmpLegacy = ".tmp"
    69â†’)
    70â†’
    71â†’// ErrNotReady is returned if the underlying storage is not ready yet.
    72â†’var ErrNotReady = errors.New("TSDB not ready")
    73â†’
    74â†’// DefaultOptions used for the DB. They are reasonable for setups using
    75â†’// millisecond precision timestamps.
    76â†’func DefaultOptions() *Options {
    77â†’	return &Options{
    78â†’		WALSegmentSize:              wlog.DefaultSegmentSize,
    79â†’		MaxBlockChunkSegmentSize:    chunks.DefaultChunkSegmentSize,
    80â†’		RetentionDuration:           int64(15 * 24 * time.Hour / time.Millisecond),
    81â†’		MinBlockDuration:            DefaultBlockDuration,
    82â†’		MaxBlockDuration:            DefaultBlockDuration,
    83â†’		NoLockfile:                  false,
    84â†’		SamplesPerChunk:             DefaultSamplesPerChunk,
    85â†’		WALCompression:              compression.None,
    86â†’		StripeSize:                  DefaultStripeSize,
    87â†’		HeadChunksWriteBufferSize:   chunks.DefaultWriteBufferSize,
    88â†’		IsolationDisabled:           defaultIsolationDisabled,
    89â†’		HeadChunksWriteQueueSize:    chunks.DefaultWriteQueueSize,
    90â†’		OutOfOrderCapMax:            DefaultOutOfOrderCapMax,
    91â†’		EnableOverlappingCompaction: true,
    92â†’		EnableSharding:              false,
    93â†’		EnableDelayedCompaction:     false,
    94â†’		CompactionDelayMaxPercent:   DefaultCompactionDelayMaxPercent,
    95â†’		CompactionDelay:             time.Duration(0),
    96â†’		PostingsDecoderFactory:      DefaultPostingsDecoderFactory,
    97â†’		BlockReloadInterval:         1 * time.Minute,
    98â†’	}
    99â†’}
   100â†’
   101â†’// Options of the DB storage.
   102â†’type Options struct {
   103â†’	// staleSeriesCompactionThreshold is same as below option with same name, but is atomic so that we can do live updates without locks.
   104â†’	// This is the one that must be used by the code.
   105â†’	staleSeriesCompactionThreshold atomic.Float64
   106â†’
   107â†’	// Segments (wal files) max size.
   108â†’	// WALSegmentSize = 0, segment size is default size.
   109â†’	// WALSegmentSize > 0, segment size is WALSegmentSize.
   110â†’	// WALSegmentSize < 0, wal is disabled.
   111â†’	WALSegmentSize int
   112â†’
   113â†’	// MaxBlockChunkSegmentSize is the max size of block chunk segment files.
   114â†’	// MaxBlockChunkSegmentSize = 0, chunk segment size is default size.
   115â†’	// MaxBlockChunkSegmentSize > 0, chunk segment size is MaxBlockChunkSegmentSize.
   116â†’	MaxBlockChunkSegmentSize int64
   117â†’
   118â†’	// Duration of persisted data to keep.
   119â†’	// Unit agnostic as long as unit is consistent with MinBlockDuration and MaxBlockDuration.
   120â†’	// Typically it is in milliseconds.
   121â†’	RetentionDuration int64
   122â†’
   123â†’	// Maximum number of bytes in blocks to be retained.
   124â†’	// 0 or less means disabled.
   125â†’	// NOTE: For proper storage calculations need to consider
   126â†’	// the size of the WAL folder which is not added when calculating
   127â†’	// the current size of the database.
   128â†’	MaxBytes int64
   129â†’
   130â†’	// Maximum % of disk space to use for blocks to be retained.
   131â†’	// 0 or less means disabled.
   132â†’	// If both MaxBytes and MaxPercentage are set, percentage prevails.
   133â†’	MaxPercentage uint
   134â†’
   135â†’	// NoLockfile disables creation and consideration of a lock file.
   136â†’	NoLockfile bool
   137â†’
   138â†’	// WALCompression configures the compression type to use on records in the WAL.
   139â†’	WALCompression compression.Type
   140â†’
   141â†’	// Maximum number of CPUs that can simultaneously processes WAL replay.
   142â†’	// If it is <=0, then GOMAXPROCS is used.
   143â†’	WALReplayConcurrency int
   144â†’
   145â†’	// StripeSize is the size in entries of the series hash map. Reducing the size will save memory but impact performance.
   146â†’	StripeSize int
   147â†’
   148â†’	// The timestamp range of head blocks after which they get persisted.
   149â†’	// It's the minimum duration of any persisted block.
   150â†’	// Unit agnostic as long as unit is consistent with RetentionDuration and MaxBlockDuration.
   151â†’	// Typically it is in milliseconds.
   152â†’	MinBlockDuration int64
   153â†’
   154â†’	// The maximum timestamp range of compacted blocks.
   155â†’	// Unit agnostic as long as unit is consistent with MinBlockDuration and RetentionDuration.
   156â†’	// Typically it is in milliseconds.
   157â†’	MaxBlockDuration int64
   158â†’
   159â†’	// HeadChunksWriteBufferSize configures the write buffer size used by the head chunks mapper.
   160â†’	HeadChunksWriteBufferSize int
   161â†’
   162â†’	// HeadChunksWriteQueueSize configures the size of the chunk write queue used in the head chunks mapper.
   163â†’	HeadChunksWriteQueueSize int
   164â†’
   165â†’	// SamplesPerChunk configures the target number of samples per chunk.
   166â†’	SamplesPerChunk int
   167â†’
   168â†’	// SeriesLifecycleCallback specifies a list of callbacks that will be called during a lifecycle of a series.
   169â†’	// It is always a no-op in Prometheus and mainly meant for external users who import TSDB.
   170â†’	SeriesLifecycleCallback SeriesLifecycleCallback
   171â†’
   172â†’	// BlocksToDelete is a function which returns the blocks which can be deleted.
   173â†’	// It is always the default time and size based retention in Prometheus and
   174â†’	// mainly meant for external users who import TSDB.
   175â†’	BlocksToDelete BlocksToDeleteFunc
   176â†’
   177â†’	// Enables the in memory exemplar storage.
   178â†’	EnableExemplarStorage bool
   179â†’
   180â†’	// Enables the snapshot of in-memory chunks on shutdown. This makes restarts faster.
   181â†’	EnableMemorySnapshotOnShutdown bool
   182â†’
   183â†’	// MaxExemplars sets the size, in # of exemplars stored, of the single circular buffer used to store exemplars in memory.
   184â†’	// See tsdb/exemplar.go, specifically the CircularExemplarStorage struct and it's constructor NewCircularExemplarStorage.
   185â†’	MaxExemplars int64
   186â†’
   187â†’	// Disables isolation between reads and in-flight appends.
   188â†’	IsolationDisabled bool
   189â†’
   190â†’	// OutOfOrderTimeWindow specifies how much out of order is allowed, if any.
   191â†’	// This can change during run-time, so this value from here should only be used
   192â†’	// while initialising.
   193â†’	OutOfOrderTimeWindow int64
   194â†’
   195â†’	// OutOfOrderCapMax is maximum capacity for OOO chunks (in samples).
   196â†’	// If it is <=0, the default value is assumed.
   197â†’	OutOfOrderCapMax int64
   198â†’
   199â†’	// Compaction of overlapping blocks are allowed if EnableOverlappingCompaction is true.
   200â†’	// This is an optional flag for overlapping blocks.
   201â†’	// The reason why this flag exists is because there are various users of the TSDB
   202â†’	// that do not want vertical compaction happening on ingest time. Instead,
   203â†’	// they'd rather keep overlapping blocks and let another component do the overlapping compaction later.
   204â†’	EnableOverlappingCompaction bool
   205â†’
   206â†’	// EnableSharding enables query sharding support in TSDB.
   207â†’	EnableSharding bool
   208â†’
   209â†’	// EnableDelayedCompaction, when set to true, assigns a random value to CompactionDelay during DB opening.
   210â†’	// When set to false, delayed compaction is disabled, unless CompactionDelay is set directly.
   211â†’	EnableDelayedCompaction bool
   212â†’	// CompactionDelay delays the start time of auto compactions.
   213â†’	// It can be increased by up to one minute if the DB does not commit too often.
   214â†’	CompactionDelay time.Duration
   215â†’	// CompactionDelayMaxPercent is the upper limit for CompactionDelay, specified as a percentage of the head chunk range.
   216â†’	CompactionDelayMaxPercent int
   217â†’
   218â†’	// NewCompactorFunc is a function that returns a TSDB compactor.
   219â†’	NewCompactorFunc NewCompactorFunc
   220â†’
   221â†’	// BlockQuerierFunc is a function to return storage.Querier from a BlockReader.
   222â†’	BlockQuerierFunc BlockQuerierFunc
   223â†’
   224â†’	// BlockChunkQuerierFunc is a function to return storage.ChunkQuerier from a BlockReader.
   225â†’	BlockChunkQuerierFunc BlockChunkQuerierFunc
   226â†’
   227â†’	// PostingsDecoderFactory allows users to customize postings decoders based on BlockMeta.
   228â†’	// By default, DefaultPostingsDecoderFactory will be used to create raw posting decoder.
   229â†’	PostingsDecoderFactory PostingsDecoderFactory
   230â†’
   231â†’	// UseUncachedIO allows bypassing the page cache when appropriate.
   232â†’	UseUncachedIO bool
   233â†’
   234â†’	// EnableSTAsZeroSample represents 'created-timestamp-zero-ingestion' feature flag.
   235â†’	// If true, ST, if non-zero and earlier than sample timestamp, will be stored
   236â†’	// as a zero sample before the actual sample.
   237â†’	//
   238â†’	// The zero sample is best-effort, only debug log on failure is emitted.
   239â†’	// NOTE(bwplotka): This feature might be deprecated and removed once PROM-60
   240â†’	// is implemented.
   241â†’	EnableSTAsZeroSample bool
   242â†’
   243â†’	// EnableSTStorage determines whether TSDB should write a Start Timestamp (ST)
   244â†’	// per sample to WAL.
   245â†’	// TODO(bwplotka): Implement this option as per PROM-60, currently it's noop.
   246â†’	EnableSTStorage bool
   247â†’
   248â†’	// EnableMetadataWALRecords represents 'metadata-wal-records' feature flag.
   249â†’	// NOTE(bwplotka): This feature might be deprecated and removed once PROM-60
   250â†’	// is implemented.
   251â†’	EnableMetadataWALRecords bool
   252â†’
   253â†’	// BlockCompactionExcludeFunc is a function which returns true for blocks that should NOT be compacted.
   254â†’	// It's passed down to the TSDB compactor.
   255â†’	BlockCompactionExcludeFunc BlockExcludeFilterFunc
   256â†’
   257â†’	// BlockReloadInterval is the interval at which blocks are reloaded.
   258â†’	BlockReloadInterval time.Duration
   259â†’
   260â†’	// FeatureRegistry is used to register TSDB features.
   261â†’	FeatureRegistry features.Collector
   262â†’
   263â†’	// StaleSeriesCompactionThreshold is a number between 0.0-1.0 indicating the % of stale series in
   264â†’	// the in-memory Head block. If the % of stale series crosses this threshold, stale series compaction is run immediately.
   265â†’	StaleSeriesCompactionThreshold float64
   266â†’
   267â†’	// FsSizeFunc is a function returning the total disk size for a given path.
   268â†’	FsSizeFunc FsSizeFunc
   269â†’}
   270â†’
   271â†’type NewCompactorFunc func(ctx context.Context, r prometheus.Registerer, l *slog.Logger, ranges []int64, pool chunkenc.Pool, opts *Options) (Compactor, error)
   272â†’
   273â†’type BlocksToDeleteFunc func(blocks []*Block) map[ulid.ULID]struct{}
   274â†’
   275â†’type BlockQuerierFunc func(b BlockReader, mint, maxt int64) (storage.Querier, error)
   276â†’
   277â†’type BlockChunkQuerierFunc func(b BlockReader, mint, maxt int64) (storage.ChunkQuerier, error)
   278â†’
   279â†’type FsSizeFunc func(path string) uint64
   280â†’
   281â†’// DB handles reads and writes of time series falling into
   282â†’// a hashed partition of a seriedb.
   283â†’type DB struct {
   284â†’	dir    string
   285â†’	locker *tsdbutil.DirLocker
   286â†’
   287â†’	logger         *slog.Logger
   288â†’	metrics        *dbMetrics
   289â†’	opts           *Options
   290â†’	chunkPool      chunkenc.Pool
   291â†’	compactor      Compactor
   292â†’	blocksToDelete BlocksToDeleteFunc
   293â†’
   294â†’	// mtx must be held when modifying the general block layout or lastGarbageCollectedMmapRef.
   295â†’	mtx    sync.RWMutex
   296â†’	blocks []*Block
   297â†’
   298â†’	// The last OOO chunk that was compacted and written to disk. New queriers must not read chunks less
   299â†’	// than or equal to this reference, as these chunks could be garbage collected at any time.
   300â†’	lastGarbageCollectedMmapRef chunks.ChunkDiskMapperRef

    40â†’// ExponentialBlockRanges returns the time ranges based on the stepSize.
    41â†’func ExponentialBlockRanges(minSize int64, steps, stepSize int) []int64 {
    42â†’	ranges := make([]int64, 0, steps)
    43â†’	curRange := minSize
    44â†’	for range steps {
    45â†’		ranges = append(ranges, curRange)
    46â†’		curRange *= int64(stepSize)
    47â†’	}
    48â†’
    49â†’	return ranges
    50â†’}
    51â†’
    52â†’// Compactor provides compaction against an underlying storage
    53â†’// of time series data.
    54â†’type Compactor interface {
    55â†’	// Plan returns a set of directories that can be compacted concurrently.
    56â†’	// The directories can be overlapping.
    57â†’	// Results returned when compactions are in progress are undefined.
    58â†’	Plan(dir string) ([]string, error)
    59â†’
    60â†’	// Write persists one or more Blocks into a directory.
    61â†’	// No Block is written when resulting Block has 0 samples and returns an empty slice.
    62â†’	// Prometheus always return one or no block. The interface allows returning more than one

Excellent! Now I have all the key information. Let me create a comprehensive summary report with all the necessary information.

## Summary

I've found and analyzed the TSDB compaction code in your fixtures. Here's a comprehensive report:

### 1. **Compactor Interface Definition**

Located in `/go/agent-index-go/testdata/fixtures/go/compact.go` (lines 52-77):

```go
type Compactor interface {
    // Plan returns a set of directories that can be compacted concurrently.
    // The directories can be overlapping.
    // Results returned when compactions are in progress are undefined.
    Plan(dir string) ([]string, error)

    // Write persists one or more Blocks into a directory.
    // No Block is written when resulting Block has 0 samples and returns an empty slice.
    // Prometheus always return one or no block. The interface allows returning more than one
    // block for downstream users to experiment with compactor.
    Write(dest string, b BlockReader, mint, maxt int64, base *BlockMeta) ([]ulid.ULID, error)

    // Compact runs compaction against the provided directories. Must
    // only be called concurrently with results of Plan().
    // Can optionally pass a list of already open blocks,
    // to avoid having to reopen them.
    // Prometheus always return one or no block. The interface allows returning more than one
    // block for downstream users to experiment with compactor.
    // When one resulting Block has 0 samples
    //  * No block is written.
    //  * The source dirs are marked Deletable.
    //  * Block is not included in the result.
    Compact(dest string, dirs []string, open []*Block) ([]ulid.ULID, error)
}
```

### 2. **LeveledCompactor Struct and Key Methods**

**Struct Definition** (lines 79-93):

```go
type LeveledCompactor struct {
    metrics                     *CompactorMetrics
    logger                      *slog.Logger
    ranges                      []int64
    chunkPool                   chunkenc.Pool
    ctx                         context.Context
    maxBlockChunkSegmentSize    int64
    useUncachedIO               bool
    mergeFunc                   storage.VerticalChunkSeriesMergeFunc
    blockExcludeFunc            BlockExcludeFilterFunc
    postingsEncoder             index.PostingsEncoder
    postingsDecoderFactory      PostingsDecoderFactory
    enableOverlappingCompaction bool
}
```

**Plan Method** (lines 249-277):
- Reads all block directories from the provided directory
- Reads meta files for each block
- Filters out blocks based on `blockExcludeFunc`
- Delegates to internal `plan()` method which:
  - Sorts blocks by MinTime
  - Checks for overlapping blocks
  - Selects non-overlapping blocks by time range
  - Compacts blocks with >5% tombstones

**Write Method** (lines 586-636):
- Creates a new block at a specific time range (mint, maxt)
- Generates a new ULID for the block
- Sets compaction level to 1
- Optionally copies parent information from base BlockMeta
- Calls internal `write()` method to persist chunks and index
- Returns the ULID of the created block (or nil if empty)

**Compact Method** (lines 485-487 and 489-584):
- Takes a list of block directories and optional already-open blocks
- Reads metadata for all blocks to compact
- Uses `CompactBlockMetas()` to merge metadata and calculate new compaction level
- Calls internal `write()` method to create the compacted block
- Returns ULIDs of created blocks
- Marks source blocks as Deletable if result has 0 samples

### 3. **How DB Triggers Compaction**

Located in `/go/agent-index-go/testdata/fixtures/go/db.go`:

**DB Struct** (lines 283-300) contains:
```go
type DB struct {
    dir    string
    locker *tsdbutil.DirLocker
    logger         *slog.Logger
    metrics        *dbMetrics
    opts           *Options
    chunkPool      chunkenc.Pool
    compactor      Compactor  // The compactor instance
    blocksToDelete BlocksToDeleteFunc
    mtx    sync.RWMutex
    blocks []*Block  // Currently loaded blocks
    // ... more fields
}
```

**Main Compaction Flow - `Compact()` Method** (lines 1414-1506):
1. Acquires `cmtx` mutex (compaction mutex)
2. Loops checking if head is compactable with delay checks
3. Creates RangeHead wrapping head data with bounded time range
4. Calls `compactHead()` to write head block
5. Calls `compactOOOHead()` to compact out-of-order head
6. Calls `compactBlocks()` to compact on-disk blocks
7. Handles WAL truncation

**compactBlocks() Method** (lines 1721-1763):
- Loops until no more compactable blocks
- Calls `compactor.Plan(db.dir)` to get list of block directories to compact
- Calls `compactor.Compact(db.dir, plan, db.blocks)` to perform compaction
- Calls `reloadBlocks()` to reload and verify blocks

**reloadBlocks() Method** (lines 1795-1902):
- Loads all block directories from disk
- Marks parent blocks as deletable based on compaction metadata
- Removes blocks outside retention policies (time and size based)
- Closes old blocks and loads new ones
- Calls `deleteBlocks()` to clean up obsolete blocks

**reload() Method** (lines 1778-1790):
- Calls `reloadBlocks()` to reload block list
- Truncates head to the max time of in-order blocks

### 4. **BlockMeta and Related Types**

**BlockMeta** (referenced but defined in Prometheus package, see lines 442-480):
- `ULID`: ulid.ULID - Unique identifier
- `MinTime`: int64 - Start of block time range
- `MaxTime`: int64 - End of block time range
- `Compaction`: CompactionMeta - Compaction tracking info
- `Stats`: BlockStats - Sample counts and statistics

**CompactionMeta** (inferred from usage, lines 457-472):
- `Level`: int64 - Compaction level (incremented each compaction)
- `Sources`: []ulid.ULID - Original source block ULIDs
- `Parents`: []BlockDesc - Direct parent blocks in compaction chain
- `Failed`: bool - Whether compaction of this block failed
- `Deletable`: bool - Whether block can be deleted
- Methods: `SetOutOfOrder()`, `SetStaleSeries()`, `FromOutOfOrder()`, `FromStaleSeries()`

**BlockDesc** (referenced in line 463):
```go
BlockDesc{
    ULID:    b.ULID,
    MinTime: b.MinTime,
    MaxTime: b.MaxTime,
}
```

**CompactorMetrics** (lines 95-157):
- `Ran`: Counter for total compactions
- `PopulatingBlocks`: Gauge for blocks being written
- `OverlappingBlocks`: Counter for vertical compactions
- `Duration`: Histogram of compaction duration
- `ChunkSize`, `ChunkSamples`, `ChunkRange`: Histograms for chunk statistics

All code is sourced from `/go/agent-index-go/testdata/fixtures/go/compact.go` and `/go/agent-index-go/testdata/fixtures/go/db.go`.

Here's how TSDB compaction works end-to-end:

---

## Compactor Interface (`compact.go:52-77`)

```go
type Compactor interface {
    Plan(dir string) ([]string, error)
    Write(dest string, b BlockReader, mint, maxt int64, base *BlockMeta) ([]ulid.ULID, error)
    Compact(dest string, dirs []string, open []*Block) ([]ulid.ULID, error)
}
```

- **`Plan`** â€” scans a directory and returns sets of block paths that should be compacted together
- **`Write`** â€” persists a single `BlockReader` (typically the in-memory Head) into a new immutable block
- **`Compact`** â€” merges multiple on-disk block directories into one new block

---

## LeveledCompactor (`compact.go:79-93`)

```go
type LeveledCompactor struct {
    ranges      []int64                              // e.g. [2h, 6h, 24h, 72h]
    chunkPool   chunkenc.Pool
    ctx         context.Context
    mergeFunc   storage.VerticalChunkSeriesMergeFunc
    enableOverlappingCompaction bool
    // + logger, metrics, encoders, etc.
}
```

**`Plan(dir)`** (`compact.go:249-277`):
1. Reads all block subdirectories and their `meta.json` files
2. Optionally filters via `blockExcludeFunc`
3. Delegates to internal `plan()` which:
   - Sorts blocks by `MinTime`
   - Detects overlapping blocks (triggers vertical/overlapping compaction)
   - Selects blocks whose combined span fits within a `ranges[]` boundary
   - Also selects blocks with >5% tombstones for early compaction

**`Compact(dest, dirs, open)`** (`compact.go:485-584`):
1. Opens/reads `BlockMeta` for each directory
2. Calls `CompactBlockMetas()` to compute merged metadata and increment `CompactionMeta.Level`
3. Calls internal `write()` to merge all series, chunks, and index into a new block
4. If the result has 0 samples, marks source blocks `Deletable` instead of writing

**`Write(dest, reader, mint, maxt, base)`** (`compact.go:586-636`):
- Used for flushing the in-memory **Head** to disk as a level-1 block
- Generates a new ULID, sets `Compaction.Level = 1`
- Calls same internal `write()` path as `Compact`

---

## DB Triggers Compaction (`db.go`)

### DB struct (relevant fields)
```go
type DB struct {
    dir       string
    compactor Compactor   // LeveledCompactor
    blocks    []*Block    // currently loaded on-disk blocks
    head      *Head       // in-memory mutable block
    cmtx      sync.Mutex  // serializes compaction runs
    // ...
}
```

### `DB.Compact()` â€” main entry point (`db.go:1414-1506`)

Called periodically by a background goroutine:

1. **Acquires `cmtx`** to prevent concurrent compaction
2. **Head â†’ disk**: loops while head is compactable (old enough), calls `compactHead()` â†’ `compactor.Write()`
3. **OOO head**: calls `compactOOOHead()` for out-of-order samples
4. **On-disk blocks**: calls `compactBlocks()`
5. **WAL truncation**: truncates WAL up to the persisted head range

### `compactBlocks()` (`db.go:1721-1763`)

```
loop:
    plan  = compactor.Plan(db.dir)      // which blocks to merge?
    uids  = compactor.Compact(db.dir, plan, db.blocks)  // merge them
    reloadBlocks()                       // reload block list
until Plan returns nothing
```

### `reloadBlocks()` (`db.go:1795-1902`)

After each compaction:
1. Scans `db.dir` for block directories
2. Marks **parent blocks** as `Deletable` (their ULIDs appear in child's `Compaction.Sources`)
3. Applies **retention** â€” removes blocks outside time/size limits
4. Closes stale `*Block` handles, opens new ones
5. Calls `deleteBlocks()` to physically remove `Deletable` blocks

---

## Key Types

```go
type BlockMeta struct {
    ULID       ulid.ULID
    MinTime    int64
    MaxTime    int64
    Compaction CompactionMeta
    Stats      BlockStats
}

type CompactionMeta struct {
    Level     int          // incremented each merge: 1 â†’ 2 â†’ 3 â€¦
    Sources   []ulid.ULID  // all original level-1 block ULIDs in lineage
    Parents   []BlockDesc  // direct parents (input to this compaction)
    Deletable bool         // safe to delete from disk
    Failed    bool
}

type BlockDesc struct {
    ULID    ulid.ULID
    MinTime int64
    MaxTime int64
}
```

---

## End-to-End Flow Summary

```
[background goroutine]
        â”‚
        â–Ľ
  DB.Compact()
  â”śâ”€â”€ compactHead()         Head (RAM) â”€â”€â–ş Write() â”€â”€â–ş level-1 block on disk
  â”śâ”€â”€ compactOOOHead()      OOO Head  â”€â”€â–ş Write() â”€â”€â–ş OOO blocks on disk
  â””â”€â”€ compactBlocks()
        â””â”€â”€ loop:
              Plan(dir)     â†’ picks e.g. three 2h blocks fitting a 6h range
              Compact(...)  â†’ merges them into one level-2 block
              reloadBlocks()â†’ marks parents Deletable, applies retention,
                              physically deletes old blocks
```

Compaction levels grow as time ranges widen: `2h â†’ 6h â†’ 24h â†’ 72h`, with each merge incrementing `CompactionMeta.Level` and carrying forward the full `Sources` lineage for deletion tracking.

result:
Here's how TSDB compaction works end-to-end:

---

## Compactor Interface (`compact.go:52-77`)

```go
type Compactor interface {
    Plan(dir string) ([]string, error)
    Write(dest string, b BlockReader, mint, maxt int64, base *BlockMeta) ([]ulid.ULID, error)
    Compact(dest string, dirs []string, open []*Block) ([]ulid.ULID, error)
}
```

- **`Plan`** â€” scans a directory and returns sets of block paths that should be compacted together
- **`Write`** â€” persists a single `BlockReader` (typically the in-memory Head) into a new immutable block
- **`Compact`** â€” merges multiple on-disk block directories into one new block

---

## LeveledCompactor (`compact.go:79-93`)

```go
type LeveledCompactor struct {
    ranges      []int64                              // e.g. [2h, 6h, 24h, 72h]
    chunkPool   chunkenc.Pool
    ctx         context.Context
    mergeFunc   storage.VerticalChunkSeriesMergeFunc
    enableOverlappingCompaction bool
    // + logger, metrics, encoders, etc.
}
```

**`Plan(dir)`** (`compact.go:249-277`):
1. Reads all block subdirectories and their `meta.json` files
2. Optionally filters via `blockExcludeFunc`
3. Delegates to internal `plan()` which:
   - Sorts blocks by `MinTime`
   - Detects overlapping blocks (triggers vertical/overlapping compaction)
   - Selects blocks whose combined span fits within a `ranges[]` boundary
   - Also selects blocks with >5% tombstones for early compaction

**`Compact(dest, dirs, open)`** (`compact.go:485-584`):
1. Opens/reads `BlockMeta` for each directory
2. Calls `CompactBlockMetas()` to compute merged metadata and increment `CompactionMeta.Level`
3. Calls internal `write()` to merge all series, chunks, and index into a new block
4. If the result has 0 samples, marks source blocks `Deletable` instead of writing

**`Write(dest, reader, mint, maxt, base)`** (`compact.go:586-636`):
- Used for flushing the in-memory **Head** to disk as a level-1 block
- Generates a new ULID, sets `Compaction.Level = 1`
- Calls same internal `write()` path as `Compact`

---

## DB Triggers Compaction (`db.go`)

### DB struct (relevant fields)
```go
type DB struct {
    dir       string
    compactor Compactor   // LeveledCompactor
    blocks    []*Block    // currently loaded on-disk blocks
    head      *Head       // in-memory mutable block
    cmtx      sync.Mutex  // serializes compaction runs
    // ...
}
```

### `DB.Compact()` â€” main entry point (`db.go:1414-1506`)

Called periodically by a background goroutine:

1. **Acquires `cmtx`** to prevent concurrent compaction
2. **Head â†’ disk**: loops while head is compactable (old enough), calls `compactHead()` â†’ `compactor.Write()`
3. **OOO head**: calls `compactOOOHead()` for out-of-order samples
4. **On-disk blocks**: calls `compactBlocks()`
5. **WAL truncation**: truncates WAL up to the persisted head range

### `compactBlocks()` (`db.go:1721-1763`)

```
loop:
    plan  = compactor.Plan(db.dir)      // which blocks to merge?
    uids  = compactor.Compact(db.dir, plan, db.blocks)  // merge them
    reloadBlocks()                       // reload block list
until Plan returns nothing
```

### `reloadBlocks()` (`db.go:1795-1902`)

After each compaction:
1. Scans `db.dir` for block directories
2. Marks **parent blocks** as `Deletable` (their ULIDs appear in child's `Compaction.Sources`)
3. Applies **retention** â€” removes blocks outside time/size limits
4. Closes stale `*Block` handles, opens new ones
5. Calls `deleteBlocks()` to physically remove `Deletable` blocks

---

## Key Types

```go
type BlockMeta struct {
    ULID       ulid.ULID
    MinTime    int64
    MaxTime    int64
    Compaction CompactionMeta
    Stats      BlockStats
}

type CompactionMeta struct {
    Level     int          // incremented each merge: 1 â†’ 2 â†’ 3 â€¦
    Sources   []ulid.ULID  // all original level-1 block ULIDs in lineage
    Parents   []BlockDesc  // direct parents (input to this compaction)
    Deletable bool         // safe to delete from disk
    Failed    bool
}

type BlockDesc struct {
    ULID    ulid.ULID
    MinTime int64
    MaxTime int64
}
```

---

## End-to-End Flow Summary

```
[background goroutine]
        â”‚
        â–Ľ
  DB.Compact()
  â”śâ”€â”€ compactHead()         Head (RAM) â”€â”€â–ş Write() â”€â”€â–ş level-1 block on disk
  â”śâ”€â”€ compactOOOHead()      OOO Head  â”€â”€â–ş Write() â”€â”€â–ş OOO blocks on disk
  â””â”€â”€ compactBlocks()
        â””â”€â”€ loop:
              Plan(dir)     â†’ picks e.g. three 2h blocks fitting a 6h range
              Compact(...)  â†’ merges them into one level-2 block
              reloadBlocks()â†’ marks parents Deletable, applies retention,
                              physically deletes old blocks
```

Compaction levels grow as time ranges widen: `2h â†’ 6h â†’ 24h â†’ 72h`, with each merge incrementing `CompactionMeta.Level` and carrying forward the full `Sources` lineage for deletion tracking.
